description_long: This is a large, rectangular outdoor air conditioning unit, typically found in urban environments attached to building exteriors. The front face features a prominent circular fan protected by a metal wire grille, with visible signs of weathering and dirt accumulation. The sides are mostly flat metal panels, one of which has a vented section for airflow, while the other side displays a small access panel and a green label. The back is plain with a small warning label in the upper right corner and two metal mounting brackets extending downward for wall attachment. The overall finish is matte and slightly worn, with a utilitarian industrial appearance. The unit is elevated off the ground by its mounting brackets, which are designed to secure it to a vertical surface.
description: A large, rectangular outdoor air conditioning unit with a circular fan and metal grille on the front, weathered metal panels, and wall-mounting brackets.
description_view_0: Side view showing a vented panel with horizontal slats and one of the metal mounting brackets.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the large circular fan behind a protective wire grille, with mounting brackets visible below.
description_view_2: Opposite side view featuring a small access panel, a green label, and a mounting bracket.
description_view_3: Rear view with a plain metal panel, a small warning label in the upper right, and both mounting brackets extending downward.
attribute_grille_pattern: Circular wire mesh grille with concentric rings and radial spokes, closely spaced for safety and airflow.
attribute_exhaust_vent_design: Single large circular vent at the front with a wire grille, side panel with horizontal slats for additional airflow.
